fault_hypothesis: The rate limit check on line 70 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), but it should be greater-than-or-equal-to (`>=`). This off-by-one error allows one more request than permitted when the number of active requests exactly equals the limit.

constraints.intent: The number of active events should not be exactly equal to the limit, as this is the incorrectly handled boundary case.
When allowing a request, the number of active events must be strictly less than the limit.
If a request is allowed, the number of remaining slots must be positive.
It is invalid to grant access (allowed=True) while reporting zero remaining slots.
